message:
chore(beads): seed 5 fresh task-type beads (CLIENT NO-EVICT/TRACKING, DEBUG infra, FAILOVER, RESET, MEMORY/LATENCY)

- s10v: CLIENT NO-EVICT + CLIENT TRACKING (REDIRECT/BCAST/PREFIX/OPTIN/OPTOUT/NOLOOP) + TRACKINGINFO
- s11v: DEBUG SLEEP/SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE/JMAP/STRINGMATCH-LEN/CHANGE-REPL-ID/QUICKLIST-PACKED-THRESHOLD test infra
- s12v: FAILOVER [TO h p [FORCE]] [ABORT] [TIMEOUT ms] state machine
- s13v: RESET full session-state cleanup audit
- s14v: MEMORY USAGE/STATS/DOCTOR/PURGE/MALLOC-STATS + LATENCY HISTORY/RESET/GRAPH/DOCTOR

All five P2/task with conformance-harness application notes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenredis-zqf8","title":"Fix fr-server help output to document --bind","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:54:25.059205393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:56:51.503667906Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:56:51.503304709Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-zrit","title":"redis parity: LPUSH variadic element ordering","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex-frankenredis","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:42:58.194018114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:47:42.736513355Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:47:42.734642925Z","close_reason":"Superseded by COMMAND INFO parity work","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-zub","title":"Fix LTRIM and LRANGE index normalization bugs","description":"LTRIM and LRANGE incorrectly handle stop indices that are less than start or less than -len, returning or keeping the first element instead of an empty list.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:15:20.278699584Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T05:47:27.164920407Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T05:47:27.162202704Z","close_reason":"Fixed LTRIM and LRANGE: clamp normalized indices before comparison to prevent negative-to-usize wrapping when indices are out of range","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenredis-s10v","title":"CLIENT NO-EVICT + CLIENT TRACKING (RESP3 invalidation push) parity audit","description":"(a) CLIENT NO-EVICT ON|OFF: prevents the eviction policy from picking this client when maxmemory is enforced. Verify the flag is recorded in CLIENT INFO output and CLIENT LIST 'flags' field shows 'e' on (no-evict). Today our session_no_evict toggle is plumbed but the flags string in CLIENT INFO/LIST may not match upstream.

(b) CLIENT TRACKING ON [REDIRECT id] [BCAST] [PREFIX p ...] [OPTIN|OPTOUT] [NOLOOP]: turn on client-side caching. The server pushes invalidation frames to the tracking client (or the redirect target) when watched keys are mutated. Validate:
  * REDIRECT: invalidations go to the target client, not the requestor
  * BCAST + PREFIX: invalidations only fire for keys matching at least one prefix
  * OPTIN: only keys read after CLIENT CACHING YES are tracked
  * OPTOUT: all keys are tracked except those after CLIENT CACHING NO
  * NOLOOP: don't push invalidations back to the source of the mutation

(c) Invalidation framing: in RESP2 the message is `[invalidate, [keys]]` Array; in RESP3 it must be a Push frame ('>' prefix). See br-frankenredis-r80v for the framing layer.

(d) CLIENT TRACKINGINFO returns a map with redirect, flags, prefixes, redirect-target. Verify shape.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 6: needs multi-client harness (one tracker, one mutator).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T04:00:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T04:00:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["client","tracking","no-evict","invalidation","resp3"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-s11v","title":"DEBUG SLEEP + DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE + DEBUG JMAP for test infra parity","description":"Several DEBUG subcommands that conformance/integration tests rely on:

(a) DEBUG SLEEP <seconds>: blocks the connection for the specified time. Used by replication tests to simulate slow commands. Today we may either no-op or block the wrong way.

(b) DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0|1: turn off active expiry for tests that want to inspect lazily-expired keys. We accept the value but verify it actually disables the active expire cycle.

(c) DEBUG JMAP: returns a JSON dump of internal state. Optional but Redis Sentinel tests use it.

(d) DEBUG STRINGMATCH-LEN <pattern> <string>: bounded-iteration glob test. Useful for hardening proptest fuzz inputs.

(e) DEBUG CHANGE-REPL-ID: rotate replication-id. Used in replication recovery tests.

(f) DEBUG QUICKLIST-PACKED-THRESHOLD: tune list quicklist node packing for tests.

(g) DEBUG STRINGMATCH-LEN: see (d).

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend core_debug.json with each combination, run live-oracle diff. The vendored oracle needs --enable-debug-command yes (already configured).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T04:00:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T04:00:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["debug","test-infra","replication","expire"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-s12v","title":"FAILOVER command + replication checkpoint semantics","description":"FAILOVER [TO host port [FORCE]] [ABORT] [TIMEOUT ms] is Redis 7.0+ replication failover orchestration. Validate:

(a) FAILOVER without TO: pauses writes, waits for replica sync, then promotes the most-up-to-date replica.

(b) FAILOVER TO host port: targets a specific replica. FORCE skips the wait-for-sync if the timeout elapses.

(c) FAILOVER ABORT: cancels an in-progress failover.

(d) FAILOVER TIMEOUT ms: bound the wait-for-sync window.

(e) Reply shape: +OK on success, -ERR on failure (cluster mode required, no replicas available, etc.).

(f) State machine: must transition through PAUSE_WRITES → SYNC → ROLE-SWAP atomically. CLIENT PAUSE WRITE prevents new writes during the window.

(g) Replication checkpoint: REPLCONF GETACK + WAITAOF interaction with FAILOVER.

Today our FAILOVER returns a generic error. Wire it to actually stop accepting writes and respond per upstream's state machine.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 6: needs multi-process master+replica harness.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T04:00:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T04:00:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["failover","replication","cluster"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-s13v","title":"RESET command full session-state cleanup parity","description":"RESET (Redis 6.2+) resets the connection to its post-handshake state. Verify everything that should be cleared:

(a) Authentication: revert to default user.

(b) Subscribed channels and patterns (PUBSUB).

(c) Transaction state: DISCARD any open MULTI.

(d) Tracking state: turn off CLIENT TRACKING.

(e) Selected DB: SELECT 0.

(f) Client name: CLIENT SETNAME ''.

(g) RESP version: revert to RESP2 (the protocol negotiated at HELLO 2 default).

(h) Read-only mode (cluster client): RESET should restore READWRITE.

(i) Reply mode: CLIENT REPLY ON.

(j) NO-EVICT/NO-TOUCH flags: clear.

Reply: +RESET (a SimpleString) on success.

Today our RESET handler clears some of these but not all. Audit each field and test against vendored redis-server.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend core_connection.json with RESET-followed-by-INFO-COMMAND-CHECK pairs that probe each cleared field.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T04:00:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T04:00:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["reset","connection","session-state"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-s14v","title":"MEMORY USAGE/STATS/DOCTOR + LATENCY family parity","description":"(a) MEMORY USAGE <key> [SAMPLES count]: returns approximate memory footprint of a key. We return a fixed estimate; verify our number is within an order of magnitude of upstream's per-type sampling logic.

(b) MEMORY STATS: returns a map of memory subsystem counters. Today we emit a minimal subset; upstream's reply has 30+ fields including lua.caches, allocator.allocated, dataset.bytes, peak.allocated, dataset.percentage. Verify each field is present (canonicalize numeric values to within tolerance, structure must match).

(c) MEMORY DOCTOR: returns a human-readable diagnostic string. Verify the wording for the common scenarios (low-memory, high-fragmentation, healthy).

(d) MEMORY PURGE: invokes allocator-specific purge (jemalloc). We no-op; verify reply +OK matches upstream.

(e) MEMORY MALLOC-STATS: returns allocator-internal stats string.

(f) LATENCY HISTORY <event>: array of latency samples for an event.

(g) LATENCY RESET [event ...]: reset latency tracking.

(h) LATENCY GRAPH <event>: ASCII-art bar chart.

(i) LATENCY DOCTOR: human-readable analysis.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend core_server.json with these MEMORY/LATENCY cases. Memory-numeric divergences need a tolerance comparator.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T04:00:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T04:00:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["memory","latency","observability","introspection"]}
